pending.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs ~line 2832), the contract violation when `requested_run_id.is_some() && requested_started_at.is_none()` is handled by logging a `tracing::warn` and returning `None`. The caller then maps `None` to a generic 'no matching task run' error, losing the diagnostic information about the contract violation. Issue 2 specifies 'Replace any debug_assert! guards on this with runtime checks that return errors in both debug and release builds.' The current approach technically prevents the wrong behavior but the error message seen by callers/operators is misleading — it says 'no matching task run' when the real problem is a violated invariant. Change the function signature to return `Result<Option<usize>, AppError>` so the contract violation can propagate as a distinct error, or at minimum change the tracing level from `warn` to `error` since this represents a programming bug in a caller, not a recoverable warning.

The `safe_plan_hash_backfill` function (fs.rs line 2608-2616) skips backfill when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None`, which means legacy open entries created before plan persistence was added will never get their `plan_hash` filled during completion, even though the current snapshot has the answer. The test `running_entry_created_before_plan_is_not_backfilled_on_revisit` explicitly asserts this behavior. However, Issue 3 acceptance criteria states 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' — this is not true for existing legacy open entries with no provenance. Add a comment in the test and near the `safe_plan_hash_backfill` None branch explicitly documenting this as a deliberate deviation from the original issue spec, referencing the ambiguity rationale, so future reviewers don't re-open this as a regression.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:3044-3055` now auto-populates new rows from the live milestone snapshot whenever the caller omits `plan_hash`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3343-3355` later treats that stored value as authoritative. That still breaks lineage when the plan changes after dispatch but before `record_task_run_start` runs: a bead dispatched under v1 can be recorded as v2 if `persist_plan` advances the snapshot first, and finalization with the later-discovered true v1 hash then fails with a false `conflicting plan_hash` error. The new comment already describes this race, so the amendment is still incomplete. Add a regression test for `dispatch under v1 -> snapshot advances to v2 -> start without plan_hash -> complete with explicit v1`, and avoid stamping the current snapshot hash onto a new row unless it is known to match the dispatched attempt.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 16 -> Plan and Implement
